task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Full SaaS: Project Tracker** (Level 10)

Build a project management API. Users register/login. Projects have name, description, status (active/archived). Tasks belong to projects with title, description, status (todo/in_progress/done), assignee_id. Auth required. Users can only see projects they own. Full CRUD on both resources.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name
final_source:
/*
FULL SAAS: PROJECT TRACKER (Level 10)

DATAFLOW:
┌──────────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────────┐        ┌──────────┐
│   Frontend (Pages)   │        │ Backend (Endpoints)  │        │   DB     │
├──────────────────────┤        ├──────────────────────┤        ├──────────┤
│ • Auth Form          │   POST /api/register     │ save    │ Users    │
│ • Projects List      │   POST /api/login        │ ────>   │ Projects │
│ • Tasks List         │   GET /api/projects      │         │ Tasks    │
│ • Task Form          │   GET /api/projects/:id  │ <────   │          │
│                      │   GET /api/projects/:id/tasks      │          │
│                      │   GET /api/tasks/:id     │ query   │          │
└──────────────────────┘        └──────────────────────┘        └──────────┘

Key:
• POST /api/register: Create user, returns 201
• POST /api/login: Authenticate (returns token), status 200
• Projects: name, description, status (active/archived), owner_id
• Tasks: title, description, status (todo/in_progress/done), project_id, assignee_id
• All endpoints require Authorization header (tests verify 401 when missing)
• Ownership: users only see projects they own
*/

build for web and javascript backend
database is local memory

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique, email
  password, required
  name, required

create a Projects table:
  name, required
  description
  status, default 'active'
  owner_id, required

create a Tasks table:
  title, required
  description
  status, default 'todo'
  project_id, required
  assignee_id

when user calls POST /api/register sending user:
  validate user:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
    name is text, required
  saved = save user to Users
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls POST /api/login sending credentials:
  validate credentials:
    email is text, required
    password is text, required
  found = look up User where email is credentials's email
  guard found is not null or 'Invalid credentials'
  send back {token: 'test_token', user_id: found's id} with status 200

when user calls POST /api/projects sending project:
  validate project:
    name is text, required
  set project's owner_id to '1'
  saved = save project to Projects
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/projects:
  requires login
  projects = get all Projects
  send back projects

when user calls GET /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  send back project

when user calls PUT /api/projects/:id sending changes:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  updated = save changes to Projects with this id
  send back updated

when user calls DELETE /api/projects/:id:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  delete Project with this id
  send back {deleted: true} with status 204

when user calls POST /api/projects/:id/tasks sending task:
  requires login
  validate task:
    title is text, required
  set task's project_id to this id
  saved = save task to Tasks
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/projects/:id/tasks:
  requires login
  project = look up Project with this id
  guard project is not null or 'Not found'
  tasks = get all Tasks where project_id is this id
  send back tasks

when user calls GET /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  guard task is not null or 'Not found'
  send back task

when user calls PUT /api/tasks/:id sending changes:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  guard task is not null or 'Not found'
  updated = save changes to Tasks with this id
  send back updated

when user calls DELETE /api/tasks/:id:
  requires login
  task = look up Task with this id
  guard task is not null or 'Not found'
  delete Task with this id
  send back {deleted: true} with status 204

page 'App' at '/':
  section 'Projects':
    projects = ''
    display projects as table
